---
title: "fdr_by"
generated: true
description: "Benjamini-Yekutieli false-discovery-rate correction."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.inference.multiple_comparisons`](../nitrix-stats-inference-multiple_comparisons.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:fdr_by -->
```python
fdr_by(p_values: Float[Array, 'n'], *, alpha: float = 0.05) -> Tuple[Bool[Array, 'n'], Float[Array, 'n']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:fdr_by -->
Benjamini-Yekutieli false-discovery-rate correction.

Valid under arbitrary dependence of the p-values. This is the
Benjamini-Hochberg step-up applied at level $\alpha / c(m)$ with the
harmonic number $c(m) = \sum_{k=1}^{m} 1/k$, equivalently the
Benjamini-Hochberg q-values scaled by $c(m)$. Use it when the p-values
may be arbitrarily (for example, negatively) dependent and the
positive-dependence assumption of Benjamini-Hochberg is unsafe; the price is
being a factor of about $\log(m)$ more conservative.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:fdr_by -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `p_values` | `Float` | `n` | — | Flat vector of `n` per-element p-values. |
| `alpha` | `float` | — | `0.05` | Target false-discovery rate defining the rejection mask. Default `0.05`. |

**Returns** `Tuple[Bool[Array, 'n'], Float[Array, 'n']]`. The rejection mask `p_adjusted <= alpha`. The Benjamini-Yekutieli adjusted p-values (q-values), enforced monotone non-decreasing in rank.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:fdr_by -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
